authoritative_sources.title: 16 CFR Part 453 - Funeral Industry Practices
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR publishes the controlling federal Funeral Rule text for funeral-provider disclosures, itemized statements, embalming representations, and required-purchase boundaries. These controls determine what family care may say or close when families ask about prices, selected services, or required purchases.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: family support intake, price or selected-services question handling, exception refusal, and resolution evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Complying with the Funeral Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal Funeral Rule regulator. This official business guidance turns the rule into practical funeral-provider workflows for price-list timing, telephone price inquiries, statement handling, and disclosure-sensitive family communication.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: family communication, disclosure question handling, support disposition, and conflict escalation
used_for: telephone and in-person price question support

authoritative_sources.title: Funeral Directors' Handbook on Death Registration and Fetal Death Reporting
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, National Center for Health Statistics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC/NCHS publishes the national vital-statistics manual for funeral directors. It defines death-registration responsibilities, demographic data collection, medical-certifier coordination, and filing dependencies that family care must track when families ask about death certificates, permits, or copy status.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: death-certificate support, vital-record blocker tracking, and resolution confirmation
used_for: blocked handling when vital-record facts are missing

authoritative_sources.title: Consumer Guide to Funeral and Cemetery Purchases
authoritative_sources.publisher: California Department of Consumer Affairs Cemetery and Funeral Bureau
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: California's funeral and cemetery regulator publishes practical public guidance on right to control disposition, price-list expectations, embalming authorization, burial, cremation, hydrolysis, cemetery practices, licensing, and complaints. It is authoritative for California and used as evidence that state and facility rules materially change family-care support decisions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: family authority review, cemetery or crematory dependency handling, complaint escalation, and jurisdiction-sensitive support
used_for: state complaint and regulator handoff context

authoritative_sources.title: VA Burial Benefits
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The VA is the official source for veterans burial and memorial benefits. It constrains family care when survivors ask about national cemetery committal, burial allowances, memorial items, or eligibility by requiring handoff rather than unsupported benefit determinations.

authoritative_sources.title: Survivor benefits
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SSA is the official public source for Social Security survivor benefits and death-reporting expectations. It is authoritative for survivor-benefit handoff language while confirming that this lane cannot decide eligibility or payment entitlement.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: survivor benefit question triage and adjacent-owner routing

authoritative_sources.title: Application of provisions of the Bloodborne Pathogens standard to Funeral and Nursing Homes
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's official interpretation explains funeral-home obligations around occupational exposure and human remains. It is relevant to family care refusal triggers when a requester asks this support lane for body handling, preparation, viewing, transport, or safety-critical instructions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: high-sensitivity exception handling and licensed-owner or safety-boundary escalation

workflow_stages.name: Family support intake and identity or authority check
workflow_stages.description: Normalize the family care ticket, decedent case ID, requester identity, relationship, authorized representative, next of kin, person authorized to direct disposition, PADD, preneed instructions, consent, contact preferences, and jurisdiction context before responding.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Record and dependency retrieval
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve the case record, arrangement summary, statement of funeral goods and services selected, death-certificate or eDRS status, disposition permit posture, cemetery or crematory dependencies, service schedule, obituary or tribute status, remains-release status, contact log, and complaint history.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported request resolution or communication preparation
workflow_stages.description: Resolve family status questions, service-detail confirmations, death-certificate copy status, aftercare follow-up, and documented next steps only when authority, consent, source records, and tenant policy support the communication.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Exception, complaint, repeat-failure, or unsupported workaround handling
workflow_stages.description: Package policy exceptions, unsupported workarounds, repeated missed commitments, complaints, disputed family authority, missing consent, conflicting source or tenant rules, and unresolved service blockers for human or adjacent owner review.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Adjacent specialist handoff
workflow_stages.description: Route funeral directing, embalming, cemetery administration, crematory operations, benefits, billing, grief support, legal, privacy, compliance, or records-release matters with the records reviewed, missing evidence, family communication posture, and next owner.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and audit trail
workflow_stages.description: Close only with a resolution note or family communication summary that states request summary, records reviewed, action taken or withheld, unresolved dependencies, next owner, communication recipient, sensitivity controls, and source-of-truth references.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: family care ticket, request summary, complaint record, contact log, preferred language, and communication history
decedent case record, first-call note, arrangement summary, service schedule, venue record, obituary or tribute proof, and aftercare task
authorized representative, next-of-kin, person authorized to direct disposition, PADD, preneed instruction, consent, release-of-information, and contact preference records
General Price List state, statement of funeral goods and services selected, payment or balance status, and documented tenant policy when relevant to the support request
death-certificate demographic intake, eDRS or vital-record portal reference, medical certifier or coroner status, disposition permit, death-certificate copy order, and registrar dependency
cremation or hydrolysis authorization, cemetery interment record, committal schedule, remains-release or urn pickup authorization, transportation note, clergy, flowers, music, and venue dependencies
escalation package, high-sensitivity record note, adjacent-specialist routing note, resolution note, family communication summary, and audit trail entry
systems_of_record: family care CRM or service-ticketing system
arrangement conference or preneed management system
electronic death-registration system or vital-record portal
cemetery management, interment-right, and committal scheduling system
payment, POS, invoicing, trust, insurance, or preneed funding ledger
obituary, tribute, event, venue, and service scheduling systems
VA, SSA, or survivor-benefit referral references
complaint, compliance, privacy, exception, and audit workflow systems

allowed: Classify family care support requests as in scope, out of scope, blocked on prerequisites, resolved, repeat-failure escalation, sensitive-record exception, or adjacent-lane handoff.
Request or validate minimum tenant facts, source records, family authority evidence, consent records, service details, and source context required to respond safely.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, customer or stakeholder communication summaries, case or family-service workflow summaries, and escalation packages.
Apply documented service or policy rules to support status updates, communication next steps, and routing without improvising exception authority.
escalate: Requester identity, authority, consent, family hierarchy, PADD, preneed instruction, jurisdiction, facility, or contact-preference facts are missing or disputed.
Death-certificate, eDRS, certifier, coroner, disposition permit, cemetery, crematory, remains-release, VA, SSA, service schedule, obituary, or payment records are missing or materially conflict.
A support issue repeats after documented follow-up commitments, includes a complaint, or requires a policy, pricing, refund, cemetery-fee, goodwill, benefits, privacy, or records-release exception.
Public sources, jurisdiction rules, cemetery or crematory requirements, preneed documents, family authority evidence, or tenant policies materially conflict.
refuse: Disclose sensitive death-care details, service changes, remains-release information, or family contact records to an unauthorized requester.
Authorize or promise embalming, preparation, cremation, hydrolysis, burial, transportation, remains release, final disposition, or safety-critical actions outside licensed or authorized owners.
Resolve legal family-authority disputes, approve payment or policy exceptions, adjudicate VA or SSA benefits, provide grief counseling, or give legal, clinical, tax, estate, or safety advice.
Mark a family care case resolved without evidence of the records reviewed, action taken or withheld, communication recipient, unresolved dependencies, and next owner.

domain_failure_modes: the lane treats family care as generic customer support and omits decedent case, authority, consent, vital-record, service, cemetery, crematory, or remains-release evidence
a family communication discloses service details, remains-release status, or records to a caller without verified authority or consent
a policy exception, refund, cemetery fee waiver, or same-day scheduling request is approved by family care instead of routed to an authorized approver
death certificate, certifier, coroner, eDRS, disposition permit, or registrar dependencies are skipped and a family is given an unsupported completion promise
a cremation, hydrolysis, burial, embalming, viewing, transportation, or remains-release request is treated as a support update rather than licensed or authorized owner work
VA, SSA, estate, insurance, or payment questions are answered as benefit or legal decisions rather than packaged for adjacent owners
repeated missed follow-ups or complaints are closed as ordinary support notes without escalation history, owner, or unresolved blocker tracking
tenant policy is applied despite conflict with public source requirements, jurisdiction rules, consent records, family authority records, cemetery or crematory requirements, or preneed documents

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Right-to-control disposition hierarchy, consent forms, remains-release rules, cremation or hydrolysis authorization, cemetery requirements, complaint procedures, death-certificate filing details, and preneed terms vary materially by jurisdiction and tenant.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The validated pack converts jurisdiction and tenant variance into mandatory runtime facts, escalation triggers, and refusal rules. It does not guess state-specific authority or form sufficiency.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenants may use different system names for family care tickets, contact logs, arrangement summaries, eDRS references, consent artifacts, cemetery records, crematory records, and complaint queues.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Runtime must retrieve tenant-specific systems of record and record locations before support resolution. The pack defines the required source-of-truth classes and blocked behavior when mappings are missing.

notes: The research output constrains the final pack to funeral, cemetery, crematory, vital-record, survivor-benefit handoff, consent, and family-authority evidence rather than generic customer care.

No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because unresolved jurisdiction and tenant-specific variance is represented as required prerequisite, escalation, or refusal handling.
